proposed_improvements.current_behavior: Prompt schema example uses type-placeholder tokens (e.g., 'integer') that produce invalid JSON and risk model reproducing them verbatim.
proposed_improvements.proposed_improvement: Instruct the code-implementor-agent to use concrete valid JSON examples in all prompt schema examples. Specifically: 'Use a real representative data example with actual values (e.g., a known player name and jersey number) rather than type placeholders like "integer" or "string" in JSON schema examples within prompt files.'
proposed_improvements.rationale: Type-placeholder JSON is not parseable and may cause model to reproduce the literal tokens in output.

proposed_improvements.current_behavior: SHA-256 stability test only checked output format (64 hex chars), not a pinned value.
proposed_improvements.proposed_improvement: Add instruction: 'When writing a SHA-256 stability test for a content file, always pin the expected hash as a module-level constant and assert equality. Never assert only format properties of the hash output.'
proposed_improvements.rationale: A hash-format-only test provides zero change detection coverage.

proposed_improvements.current_behavior: Substring presence tests used incidental matches (jersey numbers satisfying numeric range tests, single OR condition passing trivially).
proposed_improvements.proposed_improvement: Add instruction: 'When testing that a text file contains a specific phrase or range, use regex patterns that match the intended prose rather than bare substring checks that may match unrelated content.'
proposed_improvements.rationale: Naive substring tests create false confidence - the test passes even when the targeted requirement is absent.

proposed_improvements.current_behavior: No .gitattributes file was created to enforce LF line endings, leaving prompt files vulnerable to CRLF changes on Windows.
proposed_improvements.proposed_improvement: Add instruction: 'When creating text files with SHA-256 stability requirements, always add a .gitattributes entry enforcing LF line endings (text eol=lf) for those file patterns.'
proposed_improvements.rationale: Without enforced LF endings, cross-platform development silently corrupts hash-stable files.

proposed_improvements.current_behavior: Implementation did not address pre-existing divergence between function_app.py and trapi_client.py hash computation for the same prompt file.
proposed_improvements.proposed_improvement: Add instruction: 'When creating a new artifact that is referenced by multiple modules, check that all referencing modules use consistent normalization/hashing logic. Flag inconsistencies even if fixing them is out of scope.'
proposed_improvements.rationale: Cross-component hash divergence undermines audit trail and was only caught in code review.
